free_text: Results for some of the light elements and conclusions
Natural lithium contains the isotopes Li-7 and Li-6 in
the proportions 92.5/7.5. A self-supporting foil of
natural lithium made by squeezing a small piece of the

The target of Be-9 was a self-supporting foil having a
thickness of about 1 mg/cm**2 prepared by evaporation.
Natural boron contains the isotopes B-11 and B-10 in
available as deposited layers on thick platinum. It was
found possible to cleave a flake of B-11 from the
platinum having an area of about 10 mm**2 and a
thickness of about 8 mg/cm**2. This was cemented at one
corner to a gold support and used as a target.
